original: [Etalius] begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Leo III begins his reign as Pope after his election the previous day
Albert II of Habsburg becomes King of Bohemia
Spanish Crown issues the Laws of Burgos, governing the conduct of settlers with regards to native Indians in the New World
The "Zwickau Prophets" appear in Wittenberg
Dutch troops capture Madurees prince Trunudjojo in Java
England & Portugal sign Methuen-Asiento-trade agreement
Prussian forces took Olmutz, Czechoslovakia

Destruction of schooner Carolina, the last of Commodore Daniel Patterson's makeshift fleet that fought a series of delaying actions that contributed to Andrew Jackson's victory at the Battle of New Orleans.
Worst English avalanche kills 8 of 15 buried (Lewes Sussex)
Ether 1st used in childbirth in US in Jefferson, Georgia
Hawaiian Fire Department established
Battle of Chickasaw Bluffs, MS (Chickasaw Bayou)
Battle of Elizabethtown, Kentucky
Ontario & Quebec legislatures hold 1st meeting
Netherlands recognizes KingLeopold II's Congo Free State
Biddle University beats Livingstone College 5-0 in 1st black college football game, in Salisbury, North Carolina
Foundation Stone of Cathedral of St John laid (NYC)
Stanley Cup, Victoria Rink, Montreal, Quebec: Montreal Victorias beat Ottawa Capitals, 15-2
Carrie Nation's 1st public smashing of a bar (Carey Hotel, Wichita, Kansas)
"Sweet Adeline", a barbershop quartet favorite, with lyrics by Richard Husch Gerard and music by Harry Armstrong, is first sung
W B Yeats and Lady Gregory's "On Baile's Strand" premieres in Dublin
Australian cricket all-rounder Monty Noble scores 281 (369 mins with 21 fours) in NSW total 805 v Victoria in Melbourne
1st annual meeting of American Sociological Society, Providence, Rhode Island
US Iron and Steel workers begin a 3-week strike in Ohio for an eight-hour-day; they are successful as the US needs steel for armaments
The Great Poland Uprising against the Germans begins.
Albania is declared a dictatorship under Ahmed Beg Zofu
The US signs a treaty with the Dominican Republic, which supersedes that of 1907: in July, the Us had withdrawn its Marines and ended its occupation
Depot Square in Bronx renamed Botanical Square
Latkin Square in Bronx named for 1st US Jewish soldier to die in WW I
Philip Barry's play "Paris Bound" premieres in NYC
Radio City Music Hall, designed by Edward Durell Stone and Donald Deskey opens at Rockerfeller Center in Manhattan, New York City
1st youth hostel in US opens (Northfield, Mass)
German immigration officials with no explanation bar Juan Carlos Zabala (Arg), 1932 Olympic marathon champion, from entering Germany
1st American skimobiles (North Conway, NH)
Between 20,000 and 40,000 die in magnitude 8 earthquake in Erzincam, Turkey
Japan bombs Manila even though it was declared an "open city"
1st Japanese women camp (Ambarawa) goes into use
The Union of Pioneers of Yugoslavia is founded.
France transfers most of her powers in Lebanon to Lebanese government
German warship "Scharnhorst" sinks in Barents Sea
Greece: British premier Winston Churchill flies back to London
1st "Howdy Doody Show" (Puppet Playhouse), telecast on NBC
1961 NFL Draft: Tommy Mason from University of Tulane first pick by Minnesota Vikings
France performs nuclear test
Belgium & Congo resume diplomatic relations
28th Heisman Trophy Award: Terry Baker, Oregon State (QB)
Ballon d'Or: Manchester United midfielder Bobby Charlton wins award for best European football player; claims award by a single point ahead of Benfica striker Eusébio
Apollo 8 returns to Earth
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC
Belgium recognizes German DR
LA Kings start Islanders on 12 game losing streak
New North Korean constitution comes into effect
MLB Los Angeles Dodgers announce their home night games will start at 7:30 instead of 8
40th Heisman Trophy Award: Archie Griffin, Ohio State (RB)
Dear Abby show ends run on CBS radio after 11 years
FSLN seizes government hostages at a private Managua party
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Explosion at Chasnala Colliery collapses drowning 350 in Dhanbad, India
Albania constitution goes into effect
Ballon d'Or: Hamburg's English forward Kevin Keegan wins his 1st of 2 consecutive trophies as best football player in Europe; beats Barcelona striker Hans Krankl and Anderlecht winger Rob Rensenbrink
Spain becomes a democracy after 40 years of dictatorship as King Juan Carlos ratifies Spain's 1st democratic constitution
After invading Afghanistan two days earlier Soviet forces pull off a coup in Kabul, killing President Hafizullah Amin
Los Angeles left wing Charlie Simmer scores in a Kings' 3-0 shutout of the Quebec Nordiques to extend his modern day NHL goal-scoring streak to 13 straight games (ends next game)
Red Army beats NY Rangers 5-2 at Madison Square Garden
Soap Opera "Knots Landing" premieres on CBS-TV
Calvin Murphy (Rockets) begins longest NBA free throw streak of 78
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Ballon d'Or: Juventus' French midfielderMichel Platiniclaims first of 3 trophies for Europe's best football player ahead of Liverpool midfielder Kenny Dalgleish and Vejle BK striker Allan Simonsen
Propane gas fire devastated 16 blocks of Buffalo
Padres' free agent pitcher Ed Whitson signs with NY Yankees
Musical "Les Misérables" opens in the US at the Kennedy Center, Washington, D.C.
Steve Largent sets all-time NFL record for career catches when he catches his 752nd pass
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Bulgaria stops jamming Radio Free Europe after more than 3 decades
Oregon begins taking bids on NBA games

Edmonton Oilers beat the Calgary Flames, 4-1 to record their 500th all-time NHL victory; set in just 915 games for a record of 500-295-120, good for a winning % of .612; best in history upon reaching milestone
Bengals hire Dave Shula as youngest NFL coach (32)
13th United Negro College Fund raises $11,000,000
Dow Jones Industrial Average hits record 3792.93
Taliban forces retake the strategic Bagram air base which solidifies their buffer zone around Kabul.
Protestant paramilitary leader Billy Wright is assassinated in Northern Ireland.
The People's Republic of China is granted permanent normal trade relations with the United States.
Kenyan General Election: National Rainbow Coalition headed by Mwai Kibaki wins, overthrowing 39 year rule by the Kenya African National Union
Two truck bombs kill 72 and wound 200 at the pro-Moscow headquarters of the Chechen government in Grozny, Chechnya.
Radiation from an explosion on the magnetar SGR 1806-20 reaches Earth. It is the brightest extrasolar event known to have been witnessed on the planet.
Israel launches Operation Cast Lead in the Gaza Strip, beginning with an airstrike that hits 100 targets in 220 seconds killing around 250 people
The World Health Organization says 208 countries, territories and communities have reported H1N1 cases, including at least 12,220 deaths
NASA unveils plans to capture a 500 ton asteroid in 2025
29 people are killed after a bus plunged off the Huay Tong Bridge, Thailand
Iraqi forces retake IS held city of Ramadi (ISIS first captured in May)
Stage play "Peter Pan" byJ. M. Barriepremieres at the Duke of York Theatre in London
Red Sox ownerHarry Frazeeannounces they will deal any player except Harry Hooper, Hooper is sent to the White Sox after 1920 season
Emperor Hirohitoof Japan narrowly evades an assassination attempt by a Korean independence activist, Lee Bong-chang
Musical revue "Thumbs Up", including music and lyrics by Vernon Duke, James Hanley, andIra Gershwin(among others), premieres at the St. James Theatre, NYC; runs for 156 performance
Shah of PersiaMohammad Reza Pahlavideclares Persia now Iran
Mae Westperforms Adam & Eve skit that gets her banned from NBC radio
Dmitri Shostakovichcompletes his 7th Symphony in Siberia
Montgomery discusses Operation Overlord (D-day landings) withEisenhower& Bedell Smith

International Monetary Fund and The International Bank for Reconstruction and Development are formally established with signing of articles of agreement by member countries, ratifying Bretton Woods Conference findings, based on ideas of economists Harry Dexter White andJohn Maynard Keynes
Queen Julianaof the Netherlands grants independence to Indonesia
Bill Sharman(Boston) ends NBA free throw streak of 55 games
Jule Styne,Betty ComdenandAdolph Green's musical "Subways Are For Sleeping" premieres at St James Theater, NYC; runs for 205 performances
1st appearance ofThe Supremeson "TheEd SullivanShow", singing "Come See About Me"
Columbia Records releasesLeonard Cohen's debut album "Songs of Leonard Cohen"
Ballon d'Or: Borussia Mönchengladbach's Danish forward Allan Simonsen is named Europe's best football player ahead of Hamburg forward Kevin Keegan and Nancy midfielderMichel Platini
CBS Television's premiere of its version of "Les Misérables", starring Richard Jordan andAnthony Perkins
Sire Records releases "Pretenders", the debut album ofThe Pretenders, in US
AustralianDennis Lilleebecomes the leading wicket-taker in test cricket with 310 wickets
Edmonton OilerWayne Gretzkybecomes fastest NHLer to get 100 point in a season (his 38th game), with 4 goals and an assist in 10-3 win over visiting Los Angeles Kings
PopeJohn Paul IIpardons man who shot him (Mehmet Ali Agca)
Arab terrorists attacks airports of Rome and Vienna, killing 20 and wounding 110.President Reaganblames Libyan leaderMuammar Gaddafi.
Ballon d'Or: Milan 1-2-3; Dutch strikerMarco van Bastenwins his 1st of 3 best football player in Europe awards ahead of Ruud Gullit and Frank Rijkaard
American jazz singer and piano playerHarry Connick Jris caught with 9mm gun in NYC's JFK airport
Joe Sakicrecords his 1,000th career point, an assist against the St. Louis Blues

Pittsburgh Penguins center and part-ownerMario Lemieuxscores a goal and 2 assists in his return to the NHL after more than 3 years out with injury problems and lingering cancer issues; Penguins beat Toronto Maple Leafs, 5-0

"Chicago" (Best Picture 2003), film based onJohn KanderandFred Ebb's musical, directed by Rob Marshall, starringRenée Zellweger,Richard Gere, andCatherine Zeta-Jonesis released. in NYC, Los Angeles, and select North American cities
Former Pakistani Prime MinisterBenazir Bhuttois assassinated by a suicide bomber in Rawalpindi
Raheem Sterlingscores to give Manchester City 1-0 win over Newcastle United at St. James' Park; EPL record 18th consecutive win; streak ends with 0-0 draw at Crystal Palace 31/12
Luka Dončićbecomes the first player to register a 60-21-10 triple-double in NBA history as the Dallas Mavericks beat the NY Knicks, 126-121; first 60-point game in Mavs franchise history
Hagia Sophia inaugurated by theEmperor Justinian Ias an Eastern Orthodox cathedral
"Flushing Remonstrance" petition signed in the Dutch colony of New Netherland, protesting the ban on Quaker worship
